1575	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1576	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1577	///
1578	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1579	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1580	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1581	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1582	    let c = p.z.getc();
1583	
1584	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1585	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1586	    if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1587	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1588	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1589	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1590	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1591	        let cl = crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?;
1592	        // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1593	        debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1594	        // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1595	        func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1596	    } else {
1597	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1598	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1599	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1600	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1601	        let cl = parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?;
1602	        debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1603	        func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1604	    }
1605	
1606	    Ok(())
1607	}
1608	
1609	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1610	///
1611	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1612	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1613	    state: &mut LuaState,
1614	    z: ZIO,
1615	    name: &[u8],
1616	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1617	) -> LuaStatus {
1618	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1619	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1620	    state.inc_nny();
1621	
1622	    let mut p = SParser {
1623	        z,
1624	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1625	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1626	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1627	        name: name.to_vec(),
1628	    };
1629	
1630	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1631	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1632	
1633	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1634	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1635	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1636	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1637	
1638	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1639	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1640	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1641	
1642	    // C: decnny(L)
1643	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1644	    state.dec_nny();
1645	
1646	    status
1647	}
1648	
1649	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1650	// PORT STATUS
1651	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1652	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1653	//   confidence:    medium
1654	//   todos:         23
1655	//   port_notes:    13
1656	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1657	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1658	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1659	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).

2724	/// C: LClosure *luaY_parser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, Mbuffer *buff, Dyndata *dyd,
2725	///                           const char *name, int firstchar)
2726	/// Top-level entry point: parses a chunk and returns the main LClosure.
2727	/// LUAI_FUNC visibility.
2728	///
2729	/// PORT NOTE: In C, returns `LClosure *` (a GC object). In Rust (Phase A),
2730	///   we return `Box<LuaProto>` since we don't have GcRef<LuaLClosure> ready.
2731	///   Phase B will wrap this in a proper LuaLClosure / GcRef.
2732	pub fn parse(
2733	    state: &mut LuaState,
2734	    // TODO(port): z: ZIO — from lua-lex / lua-vm
2735	    // TODO(port): buff: LexBuffer — from lua-lex
2736	    dyd: DynData,
2737	    name: &[u8],
2738	    firstchar: i32,
2739	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2740	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
2741	        current: firstchar,
2742	        linenumber: 1,
2743	        lastline: 1,
2744	        t: LexToken::default(),
2745	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
2746	        fs: None,
2747	        dyd,
2748	        source: None, // TODO(port): intern `name` via state
2749	        envn: None,   // TODO(port): intern b"_ENV" via state
2750	    };
2751	
2752	    // C: cl = luaF_newLclosure(L, 1); setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)
2753	    // TODO(port): allocate LuaLClosure via state
2754	
2755	    // C: lexstate.h = luaH_new(L); sethvalue2s(L, L->top.p, lexstate.h); luaD_inctop(L)
2756	    // TODO(port): create scanner table via state
2757	
2758	    // C: funcstate.f = cl->p = luaF_newproto(L)
2759	    let main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
2760	    let mut main_fs = FuncState {
2761	        f: main_proto,
2762	        prev: None,
2763	        bl: None,
2764	        pc: 0,
2765	        lasttarget: 0,
2766	        previousline: 0,
2767	        nk: 0,
2768	        np: 0,
2769	        nabslineinfo: 0,
2770	        firstlocal: 0,
2771	        firstlabel: 0,
2772	        ndebugvars: 0,
2773	        nactvar: 0,
2774	        nups: 0,
2775	        freereg: 0,
2776	        iwthabs: 0,
2777	        needclose: false,
2778	    };
2779	    // C: funcstate.f->source = luaS_new(L, name)
2780	    // TODO(port): main_fs.f.source = Some(state.intern_str(name));
2781	
2782	    // C: luaX_setinput(L, &lexstate, z, funcstate.f->source, firstchar)
2783	    // TODO(port): lua_lex::set_input(state, &mut lexstate, z, source, firstchar)?;
2784	
2785	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)?;
2786	
2787	    debug_assert!(lexstate.fs.is_none());
2788	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.actvar.is_empty());
2789	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.gt.is_empty());
2790	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.label.is_empty());
2791	
2792	    // C: L->top.p-- — remove scanner's table
2793	    // TODO(port): state.pop();
2794	
2795	    // C: return cl — closure is on the stack, too
2796	    // TODO(port): wrap proto in LuaLClosure and return GcRef
2797	    Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!("TODO(port): parse returns placeholder")))
2798	}
2799

2801	// PORT STATUS
2802	//   source:        src/lparser.c  (1968 lines, 95 functions)
2803	//   target_crate:  lua-parse
2804	//   confidence:    medium

2806	//   port_notes:    14

2808	//   notes:         All 95 functions translated with correct logical structure.
2809	//                  184 TODO(port) stubs for cross-crate calls (lua_code::*,
2810	//                  lua_lex::*, lua_vm state allocation). Key design choices:
2811	//                  BlockCnt/LhsAssign use Option<Box<...>> chains; FuncState
2812	//                  uses Box<LuaProto> (not GcRef) for mutable access during
2813	//                  build. singlevaraux FuncState.prev chain traversal (upvalue
2814	//                  capture across closures) is a known TODO — needs recursive

2816	//                  LexState is a local stub — Phase B must unify with

561	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
562	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
563	///
564	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
565	pub struct GlobalState {

580	
581	    // C: stringtable strt — intern table for short strings
582	    // types.tsv: global_State.strt → StringPool
583	    pub strt: StringPool,
584	
585	    // C: TValue l_registry — the Lua registry (always a table once state is complete)

701	    pub memerrmsg: GcRef<LuaString>,

711

723	    /// Total live bytes allocated (GCdebt + totalbytes).
724	    ///
725	    /// C: `gettotalbytes(g)` macro → `cast(lu_mem, (g)->totalbytes + (g)->GCdebt)`
726	    /// macros.tsv: `gettotalbytes → g.total_bytes()`
727	    pub fn total_bytes(&self) -> usize {
728	        // C: cast(lu_mem, (g)->totalbytes + (g)->GCdebt)
729	        (self.totalbytes + self.gc_debt) as usize
730	    }
731	
732	    /// Returns `true` when the state has been fully initialized.
733	    ///
734	    /// C: `completestate(g)` macro → `ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)`
735	    /// macros.tsv: `completestate → g.is_complete()`
736	    ///

738	    /// We replicate the same logic: `nilvalue == Nil` means complete.
739	    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {
740	        // C: ttisnil(&g->nilvalue)

742	    }

1076:pub fn load_string(state: &mut LuaState, s: &[u8]) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {

content.content:
1020	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a single buffer slice then signals EOF.
1021	///
1022	/// C: `static const char *getS(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1023	fn make_string_reader(data: Vec<u8>) -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1024	    let mut remaining = Some(data);
1025	    move || remaining.take()
1026	}
1027	
1028	/// Load a file as a Lua chunk. Returns `LUA_OK` on success or an error code.
1029	///
1030	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadfilex(lua_State *L, const char *filename, const char *mode)`
1031	// TODO(port): uses std::fs which is banned outside lua-cli; stub for Phase A.
1032	pub fn load_filex(
1033	    state: &mut LuaState,
1034	    filename: Option<&[u8]>,
1035	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1036	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1037	    // TODO(port): open file with std::fs::File, handle BOM, skip shebang line.
1038	    // For Phase A we always fail cleanly.
1039	    let _ = (filename, mode);
1040	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
1041	        "luaL_loadfilex not implemented in Phase A (std::fs banned outside lua-cli)"
1042	    )))
1043	}
1044	
1045	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk.
1046	///
1047	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadbufferx(lua_State *L, const char *buff, size_t size, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1048	pub fn load_bufferx(
1049	    state: &mut LuaState,
1050	    buff: &[u8],
1051	    name: &[u8],
1052	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1053	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1054	    // C: LoadS ls; ls.s = buff; ls.size = size;
1055	    // C: return lua_load(L, getS, &ls, name, mode);
1056	    // TODO(phase-b): state.load expects (chunk: &[u8], name, mode) in state_stub; the reader-based loader needs a load_with_reader API match.
1057	    let _reader = make_string_reader(buff.to_vec());
1058	    let ok = state.load(buff, name, mode)?;
1059	    Ok(if ok { 0 } else { 1 })
1060	}
1061	
1062	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk (no mode argument).
1063	///
1064	/// C: `#define luaL_loadbuffer(L,s,sz,n) luaL_loadbufferx(L,s,sz,n,NULL)`
1065	pub fn load_buffer(
1066	    state: &mut LuaState,
1067	    buff: &[u8],
1068	    name: &[u8],
1069	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1070	    load_bufferx(state, buff, name, None)
1071	}
1072	
1073	/// Load a NUL-terminated byte-string as a Lua chunk.
1074	///
1075	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadstring(lua_State *L, const char *s)`
1076	pub fn load_string(state: &mut LuaState, s: &[u8]) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1077	    // C: return luaL_loadbuffer(L, s, strlen(s), s);
1078	    load_buffer(state, s, s)
1079	}
1080	
1081	// ── Meta-field and misc helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1082	
1083	/// Push the metafield `event` of `obj` onto the stack and return its type.
1084	/// If there is no metafield, nothing is pushed and `LuaType::Nil` is returned.
1085	///
1086	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_getmetafield(lua_State *L, int obj, const char *event)`
1087	pub fn get_metafield(
1088	    state: &mut LuaState,
1089	    obj: i32,

283	/// C: Dyndata — parser-local mutable lists (active vars, gotos, labels).
284	/// C stored C-style dynamic arrays (arr/n/size); Rust uses Vec.

297	/// C: BlockCnt — one nested block scope (defined in lparser.c, not header).
298	/// In C: stack-allocated, chained via raw `*previous` pointer.
299	/// In Rust: heap-allocated in an `Option<Box<BlockCnt>>` chain on FuncState.

302	    /// C: *previous — enclosing block; None at function top level.

content.text: Now I have a clear understanding. The fix is to wire `parse_stub` through a function-pointer hook on `GlobalState`, registered by `lua-cli`. Let me implement.

content.content:
1	//! Global State — port of `lstate.c` (445 lines, 25 functions) + `lstate.h` (merged).
2	//!
3	//! Manages per-thread ([`LuaState`]) and process-wide ([`GlobalState`]) Lua state:
4	//! creation, initialization, teardown, and coroutine lifecycle helpers.
5	//!
6	//! The `lstate.h` header is merged into this module per PORTING.md §1.
7	//!
8	//! # C source files
9	//! - `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lstate.c`  (445 lines, 25 functions)
10	//! - `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lstate.h`  (408 lines; struct + macro definitions merged)
11	
12	// C: #define lstate_c
13	// C: #define LUA_CORE
14	
15	// PORT NOTE: The C `LX` (thread + extra space) and `LG` (LX + global state) layout
16	// wrappers are C-only pointer-arithmetic helpers for allocating the main thread and
17	// GlobalState as one contiguous block. In Rust, `GlobalState` and `LuaState` are
18	// separate heap-allocated values linked via `Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>`. No LX/LG
19	// equivalents are needed.
20	
21	// PORT NOTE: C macro `fromstate(L)` (cast LX* from lua_State*) is C-only pointer
22	// arithmetic and is not translated. Rust owns the allocations via Rc/Box.
23	
24	use std::cell::RefCell;
25	use std::rc::Rc;
26	
27	use crate::string::StringPool;
28	pub use lua_types::error::LuaError;
29	pub use lua_types::{CallInfoIdx, StackIdx};
30	
31	/// Internal: a thin wrapper used so stubbed methods can accept either
32	/// `StackIdx` or `u32` (Phase A code mixes both). Phase B will normalise.
33	pub struct StackIdxConv(pub StackIdx);
34	
35	/// Phase-A code casts `StackIdx as i32`; provide a `From` so it compiles.
36	/// TODO(phase-b): expressions like `state.top_idx().0 as i32` should become
37	/// `state.top_idx().raw() as i32`. The non-primitive-cast error is silenced
38	/// here by promoting the StackIdx through a free-function conversion.
39	#[inline]
40	pub fn stack_idx_to_i32(i: StackIdx) -> i32 { i.0 as i32 }

content.content:
555	impl Collectable for LuaState {}
556	
557	// ─── GlobalState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
558	
559	/// Process-wide state shared by all Lua threads.
560	/// Function-pointer signature for the text-source parser, installed on
561	/// [`GlobalState::parser_hook`] by the embedder.
562	///
563	/// The implementation lives in `lua-parse`; `lua-vm` cannot depend on it
564	/// directly (that would form a cycle), so the parser is reached via this
565	/// function pointer registered at startup.
566	pub type ParserHook = fn(
567	    state: &mut LuaState,
568	    name: &[u8],
569	    firstchar: i32,
570	) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::closure::LuaLClosure>, LuaError>;
571	
572	///
573	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
574	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
575	///
576	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
577	pub struct GlobalState {
578	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
579	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
580	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
581	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
582	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
583	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,
584	
585	    // C: l_mem totalbytes — Phase D memory accounting
586	    // types.tsv: global_State.totalbytes → isize
587	    pub totalbytes: isize,

content.text:
No new warnings in my code. The fix is complete.

## Report

**Root cause**
`parse_stub` at `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:43-55` hard-coded a syntax-error return because `lua-vm` cannot depend on `lua-parse` (cyclic — `lua-parse` already imports `lua_vm::state::LuaState`). `f_parser` reached this stub for any text source and bailed before the parser was ever invoked.

**The fix**
- `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs`: added `pub type ParserHook = fn(&mut LuaState, &[u8], i32) -> Result<GcRef<LuaLClosure>, LuaError>` and a `parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>` field on `GlobalState`, initialised to `None` in `new_state`.
- `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs`: `parse_stub` now reads `state.global().parser_hook` and delegates to it when present; falls back to the prior error only when no embedder has installed one.
- `crates/lua-cli/Cargo.toml`: added `lua-parse.workspace = true`.
- `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs`: defined `parser_hook` (wraps `lua_parse::parse`, wrapping its `Box<LuaProto>` result in a `GcRef<LuaLClosure>`) and installs it on the fresh `LuaState` via `state.global_mut().parser_hook = Some(parser_hook)`.

No temporary `eprintln!()` were added; no `unsafe` introduced.

**New error**
`[err] load_string status=1: unexpected symbol` — emitted from inside `lua_parse` itself. The original "Lua text parser not yet wired (phase-b: lua-parse::parse)" no longer appears, and the next blocker is now a real parser TODO inside `lua-parse`.
